growth_records.cite: Wang C et al. Epsilon-poly-L-lysine: Recent Advances in Biomanufacturing and Applications. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.748976. [PMC8506220]
growth_records.snippet: Streptomyces lividans ZX7-pls, fed batch fermentation 120 h, glucose + yeast extract, heterologous pls from S. albulus NK660 -> 0.0025 g/L (Geng et al. (2014))
growth_records.notes: Value CITED in this review (Wang et al. 2021, Front Bioeng Biotechnol) FROM primary paper Geng et al. (2014); not measured in the review. Volumetric productivity derived = 0.0025 g/L / 120 h = 0 g/L/h. Medium given only qualitatively (glucose + yeast extract), not quantified -> medium_composition_stated:false. No growth rate, biomass, or substrate uptake reported here; epsilon-PL is the excreted product. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]
